title: ICE History Tracker Update — Apr 23, 2026
summary: The U.S. Senate voted 50-48 in the predawn hours of April 23 to adopt a $70 billion budget reconciliation resolution funding ICE and Customs and Border Protection through Trump's term — bypassing Democratic filibuster demands for use-of-force reforms triggered by the January 2026 fatal Minneapolis shootings of two U.S. citizens by federal agents. Only Sens. Rand Paul and Lisa Murkowski broke Republican ranks; the measure advances to the House with a June 1 deadline. DHS has been in partial shutdown since February 14. Separately, a KCUR/Beacon News investigation documented Kansas City International Airport (KCI) as a major ICE Air deportation hub with 130 flights in 2025, a 200%+ increase. Map updated with US Capitol legislative action and KCI Airport deportation hub markers.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Apr 22, 2026
summary: The 11th Circuit Court of Appeals vacated a preliminary injunction on April 21, allowing ICE's 'Alligator Alcatraz' detention facility near the Florida Everglades to remain open while environmental litigation continues — a significant legal win for the administration's detention expansion agenda. A federal judge in Illinois separately ruled on April 18 that the Trump administration violated the First Amendment by pressuring Facebook and Apple to remove ICE-monitoring apps used by immigrant communities, issuing a preliminary injunction protecting the 'ICE Sightings Chicagoland' group and 'Eyes Up' app. FY2026 ICE custody deaths stand at 29 — a 22-year record — with acting Director Todd Lyons departing May 31. Timeline and map updated with 'Alligator Alcatraz' location and recent legal developments.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Apr 21, 2026
summary: NPR published an investigation finding that Thomson Reuters employee Billie Little was fired after raising concerns that the company's CLEAR database — which holds license plate, Social Security, and address data under a $5M ICE contract — was being misused during the Minneapolis Operation Metro Surge that killed two people. ICE's first full-year FY2025 removal data (442,637 combined removals and voluntary returns) was released, with ICE formally setting a target of 1 million deportations annually in Congressional budget documentation. UCLA's Center for Neighborhood Knowledge published a report finding noncriminal Latino detentions increased sixfold under Trump. On April 20, DHS issued weekend enforcement announcements highlighting criminal alien arrests as part of its 'Making America Safe Again' campaign, even as TRAC data shows 70.8% of ICE detainees have no criminal conviction.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Mon Apr 20, 2026
summary: DHS Reports Weekend ICE Arrests of Human Traffickers, Rapists, and Kidnappers. The UCLA Center for Neighborhood Knowledge published 'ICE Raids and Shifting U.S.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Apr 19, 2026
summary: ICE custody deaths in FY2026 reached 29 on April 18, breaking the 22-year-old record of 28 set in FY2004; ICE released delayed 90-day reports for four detainees — Victor Manuel Diaz, Heber Sanchez Dominguez, Parady La, and Luis Nunez Caceres — missing congressional notification deadlines. Most recent death: Aled Damien Carbonell-Betancourt, 27, Cuban national found unresponsive at Miami federal detention, presumed suicide. On April 19, former immigration judges including Ryan Wood publicly condemned the Trump administration's purge of immigration court judges, with Wood stating the administration wants 'numbers, they want deportations' and describing judges being 'walked off the bench mid-decision' as unprecedented. Acting ICE Director Todd Lyons, who oversaw the enforcement surge and record custody deaths, announced his resignation effective May 31.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Sat Apr 18, 2026
summary: ICE released delayed 90-day death reports for four detainees on April 18, bringing the fiscal year 2026 total to 29 deaths — surpassing the previous record of 28 set in 2004.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Sun Apr 12, 2026
summary: White House declared the 'Era of Amnesty Is Over,' claiming asylum grant rates fell from 50%+ under Biden to 7% under Trump and over 3 million voluntary departures. The Marshall Project revealed DHS paid $170 million to local police as immigration enforcement 'incentives.' Judge Paula Xinis blocked Trump's bid to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia; AG Blanche's public comments now threaten to sink the related criminal case. DACA detentions surged with 21 federal court challenges filed in 15 months, and ICE labeled 34% of Operation Metro Surge arrests as 'collateral.' New map points added for Fort Polk (Annie Ramos detention site), the Mesa overcrowded facility, and Fort Bliss military detention.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Sat Apr 11, 2026
summary: ICE arrests have fallen below 1,000 per day following the pullout from Minnesota, with detention population dropping to 60,311 — down from 70,000+ at the Operation Metro Surge peak. A new NBC/TRAC analysis found ICE arrests of immigrants without criminal convictions rose 770% under Trump, with 70.8% of current detainees having no criminal conviction. Army Sgt. Matthew Blank's wife Annie Yaritza Ramos Alvarado was detained at Fort Polk military base and released under GPS monitoring as deportation proceedings continue, drawing national attention to enforcement actions against individuals with deep U.S. ties. ICE also moved detainees out of an overcrowded Mesa, Arizona facility before a planned congressional oversight visit. DHS data showed only 2% of February 2026 deportation cases were filed on criminal grounds.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Fri Apr 10, 2026
summary: ICE Arrests Fall Below 1,000/Day After Minnesota Pullout; Criminal Arrest Share Rises.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Thu Apr 9, 2026
summary: ICE Moved Detainees From Overcrowded Mesa Facility Before Congressional Oversight Visit.

summary: Trump Admin Reaffirms Plan to Deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Mon Apr 6, 2026
summary: US Army veteran Godfrey Wade was deported following a deportation hearing he says he was never notified about.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: ICE Ends Two-Week Airport Patrol at Southwest Florida International as Enforcement Shifts 'Quieter'.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: ICE Shifts to 'Quieter' Enforcement Approach After Minnesota Surge Backlash. The Department of Homeland Security announced on April 4 that two ICE officers were sent to the hospital after an individual under arrest attempted to dangerously evade custody.

title: ICE History Update — Apr 01, 2026
summary: Markwayne Mullin replaced Kristi Noem as DHS Secretary as the DHS partial shutdown reached Day 46 with Congress on two-week recess. Jose Guadalupe Ramos-Solano died at Adelanto ICE Processing Center on March 25 — the 14th ICE custody death of 2026 and the 47th since January 2025; ICE has stopped paying for third-party medical care since October 2025. On March 31, the U.S. flew 17 migrants to El Salvador's CECOT prison despite a federal court TRO, prompting a DOJ whistleblower to allege knowing violation of the court order. A Mass Deportation Coalition called for 'Phase II' enforcement targeting worksites and financial records. Judge Dolly Gee barred ICE from re-detaining a cognitively disabled man who had been deported without understanding the process.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: On March 31, the United States flew 17 individuals it alleged — without presenting individual evidence — were members of Tren de Aragua and MS-13 on U.S. LAist published an analysis on March 31 of ICE enforcement data showing over 14,000 arrests in the greater Los Angeles area in 2025 — three times the 4,681 arrests in 2024. DHS issued a 'Make America Safe Again' press release on March 31 announcing the arrest of multiple undocumented individuals with violent criminal records. On March 31, during a 'No Kings' demonstration in Honolulu, Hawaii, footage emerged showing a man wearing ICE-style gear being attacked by protesters.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: TSA Workers Receive Back Pay — Airport Wait Times Plummet From 4+ Hours to Under 30 Minutes. DHS Announces ICE Agent Rammed by Vehicle During Arrest Attempt in Sacramento. 14th ICE Custody Death of 2026 Publicly Confirmed: Jose Guadalupe Ramos-Solano Dies at Adelanto.

title: ICE History Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: The DHS partial government shutdown entered its 44th day on March 29, 2026, becoming the longest partial shutdown in U.S. history after Congress left for a two-week recess without resolution. The Senate passed a bill funding DHS except ICE and CBP, but the House rejected it in a 213–203 vote. 'No Kings' protests drew over 100,000 people to the Minnesota State Capitol and resulted in protesters breaching the front gate of the Portland ICE facility, prompting a riot declaration and three arrests. At least 46 people have died in ICE custody since the start of the Trump second term in January 2025, with FY2026 on pace to be the deadliest ICE custody year on record — including a death at Fort Bliss ruled a homicide. ICE now holds over 70,000 people in detention, up 89% from the start of the Trump administration, as the administration pursues a goal of 100,000 detention beds.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Sat Mar 28, 2026
summary: Hundreds of thousands of Americans participated in 'No Kings' rallies across the country on March 28, 2026, with over 100,000 demonstrators at the Minnesota State Capitol alone. Following the Portland 'No Kings' march at Tom McCall Waterfront Park on March 28, a subset of protesters moved to the ICE field office building in South Portland. In a highly unusual late-night session, the Senate passed a bill at 2:20 a.m.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Fri Mar 27, 2026
summary: Senate Passes DHS Funding Bill Excluding ICE — House Immediately Rejects It. Organizers expected over 100,000 demonstrators at the Minnesota State Capitol in St. A Washington State immigrant rights nonprofit reported that its Deportation Defense Hotline received over 10,000 calls in 2025 — more than double 2024 volumes. DHS Announces ICE Arrests of Individuals Convicted of Murder, Child Pornography, Drug Trafficking.

title: ICE History Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: ICE detention reached a record high of approximately 73,000 people in late March 2026 — an 84 percent increase from January 2025 — with over 70 percent of detainees holding no criminal conviction. The DHS partial government shutdown entered its 41st day as Senate negotiations stalled, with TSA warning of potential airport closures and the highest wait times in agency history after losing over 480 officers. A Vermont ICE raid on March 11 in South Burlington drew national attention after ICE admitted in court filings that the operation began with a case of mistaken identity, leading to the detention of three immigrants not named in any warrant — all of whom were subsequently released by federal judges. ICE also conducted high-profile criminal enforcement operations across Dallas, Chicago, Salt Lake City, and Long Island during the week of March 20–26, while deploying agents to 14 airports to fill TSA screening gaps. Senate Democrats proposed ICE accountability measures including mandatory body cameras, judicial warrant requirements, and sensitive location restrictions as conditions for restoring DHS funding.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: TSA Official Warns of Airport Closures and World Cup Risk as DHS Shutdown Hits 40 Days. Two weeks after the March 11 ICE raid on a South Burlington, Vermont home, all three detained immigrants had been released from federal custody.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Tue Mar 24, 2026
summary: DHS announced the arrest of Carlos Aguilar Reynoso, a criminal alien from Guatemala, charged with forcibly raping a 5-year-old girl on Long Island, New York. ICE admitted in a new court filing that the March 11 South Burlington, Vermont raid on Dorset Street began with a case of mistaken identity. Talks to fund the Department of Homeland Security intensified on March 24 but remained stalled as airport wait times continued to be unpredictable.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Mon Mar 23, 2026
summary: DHS announced ICE lodged an arrest detainer requesting that politicians not release Jesus Alejandro Ramirez-Padilla, a Mexican criminal alien charged with attempted murder in Salt Lake City, Utah. ICE arrested several gang members and undocumented individuals involved in a vehicle theft ring in Dallas, Texas. Federal immigration agents were deployed to patrol John F.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Sun Mar 22, 2026
summary: ICE lodged an arrest detainer for Jose Medina-Medina, a Venezuelan criminal alien arrested in connection with the killing of 18-year-old Sheridan Gorman in Chicago, Illinois.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Sat Mar 21, 2026
summary: DHS law enforcement arrested two individuals wanted in Mexico for murder and child sex crimes.

title: ICE History Tracker Update — Fri Mar 20, 2026
summary: ICE released a DHS press statement announcing arrests of individuals described as murderers, pedophiles, rapists, and other violent criminals. One week after the March 11 ICE raid on a South Burlington, Vermont home on Dorset Street, one of the three detained immigrants was released while two remained in custody.
